Design System v2.2 — CLC Inspection Tool

Sumplexity Design System

A comprehensive design language for building precise, authoritative regulatory compliance tools. Every component is purpose-built for the CLC inspection workflow.

Public Sans / Institutional Modern / UK English

Brand Identity

Logo Hierarchy

Transparent backgrounds on all variants. Choose based on display size and context.

Large — With Tagline

Hero sections, letterheads, report headers

Medium — Name Below

Navigation, footers, medium containers

Small — Icon Only

Favicons, button icons, badges, mobile nav

Horizontal Lockup

Findings & Evidence

Finding Severity

CriticalAttentionGuidanceGood Practice

Evidence Strength & Review Status

Strength

Strong Evidence Supported Indicative

Review

Accepted Rejected Unreviewed Previously Flagged

Finding Cards

Practice-wide risk assessment does not cover overseas beneficial ownership
CriticalAML Code s.3.2 Strong System Previously Flagged
Unreviewed

Regulatory reference: CLC AML Code s.3.2 requires practice-wide risk assessments to include all categories of risk including overseas beneficial ownership structures.

What was found: The firm's PWRA document contains risk categories for domestic transactions, PEPs, and source of funds, but makes no reference to overseas beneficial ownership or complex ownership structures.

Comprehensive staff AML training programme with quarterly assessments
Good PracticeAML Code s.8.1 Strong System
Accepted
Possible inconsistency in client account reconciliation dates
LeadAccounts Code s.2.4 Indicative

What was noticed: The accountant's report references reconciliation dates in March and May, but no April reconciliation is evident.

Suggested action: Request the April reconciliation statement from the practice.

Document Evidence Underlines

Coloured underlines on source text. Red contradicts, amber needs judgment, green satisfies.

The practice maintains a comprehensive complaints handling procedure that is prominently displayed in the client care letter and is reviewed annually by the HoLP.

However, the practice-wide risk assessment does not reference overseas beneficial ownership structures or trusts, despite handling 12 transactions involving offshore entities.

The client account reconciliation for April 2025 could not be located in the submitted documentation, though the accountant's report references monthly reconciliation as standard.

Document Viewer

Split View with Evidence Underlines

Source document left, findings panel right. Clickable document grid for fast switching.

AML_Policy_2025_v3.pdf
AML_Policy_2025_v3.pdf1
General_Firm_Policy.pdf2
Client_Account_Q1.pdf3

1. Practice-Wide Risk Assessment

Harris & Webb Conveyancing LLP has conducted a practice-wide risk assessment in accordance with the MLR 2017.

Risk categories identified:

— Politically exposed persons (PEPs) and their associates

— Source of funds verification for all transactions above threshold

— Domestic property transactions involving chain completion

The assessment does not reference overseas beneficial ownership structures, trusts, or offshore entities, despite handling overseas-linked transactions.

2. Client Due Diligence

The firm maintains electronic verification records for all clients through a third-party provider.

Enhanced due diligence procedures reference the 2017 Regulations but do not include the 2022 amendments regarding virtual assets.

Accordion

Anti-Money Laundering Code

Covers practice-wide risk assessments, client/matter risk assessments, CDD/EDD, source of funds, SAR reporting, sanctions checks, MLRO arrangements, and staff training. This is the code area most commonly associated with non-compliance findings.

Accounts Code

Client account management, monthly reconciliations, aged balances, proper separation of funds, interest handling.

Complaints Code

Internal complaints procedure, complaints log, Legal Ombudsman referral, vulnerability considerations.

Modals

Regenerate Report?

Regenerating will replace the current report. Any manual edits will be lost. Export first if you have made significant changes.

Empty States

No documents uploaded

Upload case documents to begin the compliance review

No inspection history

History tracks every inspection of this practice. Recurring issues will be flagged from the next inspection.
